description: Stable RoP-internal identifier for a research resource (study, data repository, biorepository, data catalog, code repository, or composite resource). Format: 'RoP-Resource:NNNNNNN'. Distinct from rop_accession (which is a CDE definition identifier) — ResourceID identifies a *resource* (PPMI, ADNI, AMP-PD, etc.) that produces or hosts data described by RoP CDEs.

metadata_.stability: never recycled; deprecated resources retain their accession with is_active=false
metadata_.rationale: Resource-level identity is the join key for every other resource-level anchor in this tier and for cross-references from data assets, summary stats datasets, and individual external dataset linkages.

description: Full, human-readable name of the resource. Examples: 'Parkinson's Progression Markers Initiative', 'Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative', 'Accelerating Medicines Partnership Parkinson Disease Knowledge Platform'.
Short identifier for the resource (e.g., 'PPMI', 'ADNI', 'AMP-PD', 'GP2', 'NACC', 'DIAN', 'ROS', 'MAP'). Useful as a join key against external datasets that reference resources by abbreviation.

Categorization of the resource. JSON array (not delimited string) to handle composite resources without ordering ambiguity. CARD Catalog's comma-delimited 'Resource Type' field had non-normalized order ('Data Repository, Biorepository' vs 'Biorepository, Data Repository'); JSON array eliminates this ambiguity.

metadata_.rationale: Resources commonly span multiple categories; PPMI is both a Study and (via LONI) effectively a Data Repository. Array-with-controlled-values captures this without delimiter or order ambiguity.

description: Free-text description of the resource — what it is, what data it produces, what its intended use is. One paragraph recommended.
High-level data modalities offered by the resource. JSON array of controlled values (replaces CARD Catalog's comma-delimited string to avoid splitting ambiguity).

Detailed modality descriptors (free text array). Examples: 'MRI', 'PET-amyloid', 'PET-tau', 'CSF biomarkers', 'WGS', 'genotyping array', 'bulk RNA-seq', 'snRNA-seq', 'somatosensory testing'. Free text because granular modalities evolve faster than a controlled vocabulary can keep up.

Diseases or conditions covered by the resource. JSON array; each entry pairs a free-text name with a controlled vocabulary code (Mondo, OMIM, HPO, MeSH preferred). Replaces CARD Catalog's free-text-only convention which mixed comma and semicolon delimiters within and across rows.

Structured sample size representation. JSON object with total participant count and optional case/control breakdown. Replaces CARD Catalog's free-text 'Sample Size' field which was not numeric and varied in convention ('766 participants (664 cases, 102 controls)' vs 'Not applicable' vs '~50,000').

schema.total_n: integer total participant count; null when not applicable
schema.cases_n: integer cases; null when not case-control
schema.controls_n: integer controls; null when not case-control
schema.as_of_date: ISO date the count was current
schema.is_estimate: boolean — true when the count is approximate or rounded
schema.notes: free text for unusual ascertainment patterns

description: Primary access URL for the resource. The canonical landing page. Additional URLs (alternative portals, archived snapshots, region-specific mirrors) go in ResourceAlternativeURLs.

Additional URLs associated with the resource (alternative portals, archived snapshots, region-specific mirrors). JSON array of URL strings. Replaces CARD Catalog's Python-list-literal-string convention which required ast.literal_eval to parse — JSON array is the standard structured representation.
Numeric FAIR-compliance score on a 0-9 scale (carried over from CARD Catalog convention). Higher is better. The component metrics that contribute to the score are recorded in ResourceFAIRIssues.

metadata_.source: Carried over from CARD Catalog scoring methodology

description: Specific FAIR shortcomings flagged for this resource. JSON array of tag strings. Common tags from CARD Catalog: 'No Version Info', 'No Environment Spec', 'No Container', 'No Dependencies', 'No README', 'No DOI', 'No License', 'No Persistent Identifier', 'No Metadata Standard', 'No DUA', 'Authentication-Walled', 'Region-Locked'.
Free-text reviewer commentary on FAIR adherence. One- to two-sentence note from the curator who scored the resource.

Person(s) who reviewed and curated this resource record. JSON array of curator entries with structured identity. Replaces CARD Catalog's free-text 'Reviewer' field that used slash-delimited initials (e.g., 'Mette/Pietro') for joint review.
Date the resource was first added to the catalog. ISO-8601 date.
Date this resource record was last reviewed or updated. Distinct from the resource's own internal release/version date.

How this resource record was created. Distinguishes hand-curated from automatically discovered records, supporting the two-source model that CARD Catalog uses (manual-curation vs automated-enrichment-discovery).

value_definitions.manual-curation: Hand-vetted by a curator from inception
value_definitions.automated-discovery: Discovered by the enrichment pipeline; awaiting human review (analogous to CARD Catalog's new_corpus table)
value_definitions.automated-discovery-promoted-to-curated: Originally discovered automatically, then human-reviewed and promoted to the curated set
value_definitions.imported-from-external-catalog: Imported from an external catalog (CARD Catalog, BioPortal, etc.) with provenance preserved
value_definitions.user-submitted: Submitted by an external user via the proposal workflow

description: Tier 8 DataAssetURI references owned or hosted by this resource. JSON array of asset URIs. Closes the loop between resource-level metadata and the binary data the resource provides — a cohort declaring data assets in Tier 8 can resolve which resource hosts each asset by joining on this field.

Tier 9 summary statistics datasets produced by analyses on this resource's data. JSON array of SummaryStatsResultsURI references. Enables traversal from resource → derived analyses for downstream meta-analysis discovery.

metadata_.format: JSON array of summary stats URIs

description: Publications associated with this resource. JSON array of {pmid, doi, pmc_id, title, year, citation_type} entries. Citation type distinguishes the publication's role: produced-by-resource (the resource's own publications), uses-resource (independent papers using the resource's data), method-paper, or cohort-description.

Code repositories associated with this resource. JSON array of {url, owner, languages, purpose, fair_score, fair_issues} entries. Purpose distinguishes processing-pipeline, analysis-code, data-loader, web-portal, or other.

schema_per_entry.fair_score: 0-9 FAIR score for the repository (per CARD Catalog convention)
schema_per_entry.fair_issues: JSON array of FAIR issue tags

metadata_.rationale: Improves on CARD Catalog's flat code table by structuring per-repository attributes consistently. The 'YES/NO/UNCLEAR' biomedical relevance verdict is preserved from CARD methodology.

description: When this resource is derived from or aggregates other resources (AMP-PD aggregates PPMI + others; Path-ND uses NACC, GP2, PPMI, ADSP-PHC), the parent ResourceID values. JSON array. Enables provenance traversal: any analysis on a derived resource can be traced back to contributing primary resources.

When the resource is a cell-line catalog (analogous to iNDI), structured metadata about the cell-line collection. JSON object with parental_line, genome_assembly, distribution_route, and per-line counts. Captures the iNDI-style 'mostly-constant fields' (KOLF2.1J parental, GRCh38, JAX distribution) without forcing them into per-row anchors.

schema.genome_assembly: Reference assembly all variants are called against
schema.distribution_route: Procurement URL or distributor name (e.g., 'JAX Mice & Services')
schema.n_lines_total: integer total cell lines in collection
schema.n_lines_engineered_controls: integer engineered control lines (no associated condition)
schema.edit_types: JSON array of edit types in the collection (SNV, DEL, REV, HALO, KI, PTC, INDEL, etc.)
schema.guide_indexing_scheme: Description of CRISPR guide naming convention if relevant

description: Reference to the Tier 7 governance row(s) that apply to this resource. Most resources inherit governance from a parent cohort, but resources can carry their own governance (e.g., the resource is a public catalog with open access while the underlying cohort is controlled-access). JSON array of governance row references.

metadata_.format: JSON array of governance row identifiers

description: When ResourceProvenanceSource is 'imported-from-external-catalog', the source catalog and original record identifier. Preserves CARD Catalog → RoP migration provenance and supports bidirectional traceability.
